title: Clibadium erosum
taxonomy: Clibadium erosum is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising over 23,000 species. Clibadium erosum is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the tribe Heliantheae, which includes many other species of daisies and sunflowers.
uses: Clibadium erosum, a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are used in various remedies to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating wounds and reducing swelling. Clibadium erosum is also used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Additionally, its leaves have been used as a natural antiseptic and to treat respiratory problems.

properties: Clibadium erosum, also known as ahuaca or pescado falso, is a plant used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin infections. constituents: Clibadium erosum contains flavonoids and other compounds that are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is also rich in phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Studies have identified various sesquiterpenes in Clibadium erosum, including erosum A and B, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's active constituents may also include alkaloids and terpenoids, although more research is needed to confirm their presence and effects.
